briefs.title: Burnaby shelter at 3020 Gilmore requires Good Neighbour and Operational Plans before occupancy approval
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has mandated that BC Housing and the shelter operator submit a Good Neighbour Plan and Operational Management Plan to the City's satisfaction before occupancy is approved for the 3020 Gilmore diversion shelter.
briefs.action_required: If operating near 3020 Gilmore, monitor City approval of the Good Neighbour and Operational Plans before shelter opens.

briefs.title: Burnaby rezones 8265–8303 10th Avenue under Bylaw #14780, final adoption complete
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council granted final adoption to Bylaw No. 14780, rezoning properties at 8265–8303 10th Avenue under REZ #25-08, amending the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965.
briefs.action_required: Check if your property or planned development at 8265–8303 10th Avenue is affected by the new zoning designation.

briefs.title: Burnaby Procedure Bylaw 2024 amended by Council with three readings passed
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council passed all three readings of Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026 to the Burnaby Procedure Bylaw 2024, updating the rules governing how Council conducts its meetings and proceedings.

briefs.title: Burnaby Plumbing Permit Fees Updated — Bylaw #14800 Passes Third Reading
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council passed all three readings of Bylaw #14800, amending the Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw to update plumbing permit fees. Businesses applying for plumbing permits in Burnaby will now be subject to the revised fee schedule.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated Burnaby plumbing permit fee schedule before submitting any new plumbing permit applications.

briefs.title: Burnaby Bear Spray Bylaw Amendment Passes Three Readings — Enforcement Now Enabled
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council passed all three readings of Bylaw #14799, amending the Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 to include bear spray regulation under the city's bylaw notice enforcement framework.
briefs.action_required: Review Burnaby's updated bear spray rules to ensure your business operations and staff comply.
briefs.industries: Retail, Grocery & Food Retail, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Burnaby Business Licence Bylaw amended to regulate bear spray sales
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council passed all three readings of Bylaw #14798, amending the Business Licence Bylaw 2017 to add regulations governing the sale of bear spray.
briefs.action_required: Review the amended Burnaby Business Licence Bylaw #14798 to confirm your bear spray sales practices comply.

briefs.title: Burnaby North Road BIA Bylaw 2026 passes all three readings at Council
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has passed all three readings of Bylaw #14797, formally establishing the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area for 2026. Businesses within the BIA boundary will be subject to the levy and governance structure set out in the bylaw.
briefs.action_required: Confirm whether your business falls within the Burnaby North Road BIA boundary and review your 2026 BIA levy obligations.

briefs.title: Burnaby rezones 7305 & 7317 Buller Ave — bylaw passes first and second reading
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council gave first and second reading to Bylaw No. 14794, initiating the rezoning of 7305 and 7317 Buller Avenue under REZ #25-07. The bylaw must still pass third reading and adoption before taking effect.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Burnaby Council agendas for third reading and adoption of Bylaw No. 14794 if you have interests at this site.

briefs.title: Burnaby Zoning Bylaw text amendment passes first and second reading
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council gave first and second reading to Bylaw No. 14792, a text amendment to the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965. The bylaw must still pass third reading and adoption before taking effect.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Burnaby Council agendas for third reading and adoption of Bylaw No. 14792 if your property or development plans may be affected.

briefs.title: Burnaby Football Club's sports field request referred to staff for strategy plan inclusion
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council referred the Burnaby Football Club's delegation request for sports field access to staff, to be considered within the ongoing sports field strategy plan.

briefs.title: Stoney Creek watershed development permits may be paused pending staff review
briefs.summary: Council has referred a request to create watershed protections for Stoney Creek and pause new development approvals to staff for a formal report.
briefs.action_required: Monitor staff report outcomes if you have active or planned development projects near the Stoney Creek watershed.

briefs.title: Stoney Creek construction impact mitigation measures sent to staff for feasibility review
briefs.summary: Council has referred the Environment Committee's recommendations on construction impact mitigation measures for Stoney Creek to staff for a feasibility review and a future report.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the upcoming staff report for any new construction requirements near Stoney Creek that may affect your operations.

briefs.title: 2026 Active Transportation Education and Promotion Initiatives Approved by Council
briefs.summary: Council has approved active transportation education and promotion initiatives for 2026, as outlined in the Transportation Committee report. Details of specific programmes and funding were not disclosed in the announcement.

briefs.title: 49-Storey Mixed-Use Tower at Springer Avenue Advances to Rezoning Bylaw Stage
briefs.summary: Council approved preparation of a rezoning bylaw for a 49-storey mixed-use residential and retail tower at 2209 & 2225 Springer Avenue, including a housing agreement and in-principle approval of a City-owned land sale.
briefs.action_required: Monitor first and second reading of the rezoning bylaw if your business operates near or on Springer Avenue.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Burnaby Lake Village Master Plan rezoning bylaw advanced for updated height, massing, and open space rules
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has authorized preparation of a rezoning bylaw to update the Burnaby Lake Village Conceptual Master Plan, revising height, massing, and open space provisions in line with OCP 2050. The bylaw will proceed to first and second reading.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Burnaby Council agendas for first and second reading of the rezoning bylaw to submit input.

briefs.title: 20-Storey Market Rental Tower at 5058 Imperial Street Moves to Bylaw Stage
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has authorized preparation of a rezoning bylaw for a 20-storey mixed-use market rental development at 5058 Imperial Street, advancing it to first and second reading.
briefs.action_required: Monitor upcoming public hearings for REZ #25-13 if your business or property is near 5058 Imperial Street.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Real Estate

briefs.title: City-owned EV charging stations switch to energy-based user fees under new bylaw amendments
briefs.summary: Council approved energy-based user fees for City-owned public EV charging stations, amending the Parking Meter, Consolidated Fees, and Bylaw Notice Enforcement bylaws to implement the new pricing structure.
briefs.action_required: Review updated EV charging fee schedules if your business relies on or manages City-owned charging stations.

briefs.title: On-Street Public Pay Parking Fees Increased by Council Approval
briefs.summary: Council has approved an increase to on-street public pay parking fee rates, as outlined in the Financial Management Committee report. Businesses relying on customer street parking should expect higher costs for visitors.
briefs.action_required: Review updated parking fee rates and communicate changes to customers who rely on on-street parking.

briefs.title: Heritage Alteration Permit approved for chimney work at W.J. Mathers House, Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council approved a Heritage Alteration Permit allowing chimney alterations at the W.J. Mathers House 'Altnadene' at 6490 Deer Lake Avenue.

briefs.title: City renews $670,000 Guardteck security contract for municipal locations and special events
briefs.summary: Council approved a one-year contract renewal with Guardteck Security Corp to provide security officer services at City locations and special events, valued at an estimated $670,000 including taxes.

briefs.title: Council renews $162,400 portable toilet contract with Jiffy John Rentals
briefs.summary: Municipal council approved a contract renewal with Jiffy John Rentals Ltd. for portable rental toilets at an estimated cost of $162,400 including taxes.

briefs.title: Burnaby Council authorizes up to 84 outdoor events at Deer Lake, Central Park, Swangard in 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has granted noise bylaw authorization for up to 84 outdoor events at Deer Lake Park, Central Park, and Swangard Stadium, running March 15–November 1, 2026.
briefs.action_required: If planning an event at these venues in 2026, confirm your event falls within the authorized 84-event allocation.

key_dates.label: Burnaby noise bylaw outdoor event season opens — Deer Lake, Central Park, Swangard
Burnaby noise bylaw outdoor event season closes — Deer Lake, Central Park, Swangard

briefs.title: Burnaby Council approves Option B design for Landmark Burnaby Sign initiative
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has authorized staff to proceed with the Option B design for the Landmark Burnaby Sign initiative. This is a municipal branding and public infrastructure decision.

briefs.title: Burnaby suspends $63,005 FCM membership dues until Big City Mayors' Caucus seat secured
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has suspended payment of its $63,005.33 Federation of Canadian Municipalities membership dues, conditional on Burnaby being granted a seat on the Big City Mayors' Caucus.

briefs.title: Temporary Use Permit renewed for commercial fleet vehicle parking at 7000 Lougheed Highway
briefs.summary: Council approved the renewal of TUP #26-01, allowing commercial fleet vehicle parking and rentals to continue operating at 7000 Lougheed Highway.
briefs.action_required: Confirm your operations at 7000 Lougheed Highway comply with the renewed TUP #26-01 conditions.

briefs.title: Antenna variance permit at 9001 University Crescent sent back for further staff review
briefs.summary: Council has referred DVP #25-02 — a development variance permit for building face coverage and height variances for antenna equipment at 9001 University Crescent — back to staff for additional review.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the permit application status and await staff's revised recommendation before proceeding with antenna installation.

briefs.title: Antenna variance permit at 8888 University Drive sent back for further staff review
briefs.summary: Council referred DVP #25-01 back to staff for further review. The application sought variances for building face coverage and height limits for antenna equipment at 8888 University Drive.
briefs.action_required: Monitor staff review outcomes if your operations depend on antenna infrastructure or this development site.

briefs.title: Statutory right-of-way approved at 3860 Sperling Avenue for utility access to Burnaby Lake Recreation Centre
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council approved a statutory right-of-way over part of 3860 Sperling Avenue, granting third-party utility providers access to service the Burnaby Lake Recreation Centre project.

briefs.title: City-wide Natural Hazard DPA launched; new construction permits paused in Lower Westridge
briefs.summary: Council initiated a city-wide Natural Hazard Development Permit Area program, approved $120,000 for a Lower Westridge hazard assessment, and asked the Province to pause new construction permits in the area pending results.
briefs.action_required: If you plan to build in Lower Westridge, expect permit delays until the hazard assessment is complete.

briefs.title: Burnaby Retailers Must Now Comply With Bear Spray Sales Regulations Under Amended Bylaws
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has amended the Business Licence Bylaw and Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw to regulate the retail sale of bear spray, introducing new compliance requirements for sellers.
briefs.action_required: Review the amended Burnaby Business Licence Bylaw to ensure your bear spray sales practices meet the new requirements.

briefs.title: Burnaby North Road BIA renewed for 2026–2031 with new bylaw incoming
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has authorized a five-year renewal of the North Road Business Improvement Association (2026–2031) and directed the City Solicitor to prepare the enabling bylaw.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the new BIA bylaw and confirm your business's levy obligations for the 2026–2031 term.

briefs.title: Burnaby plumbing permit fees amended under Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council approved minor amendments to plumbing permit fees under the Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw and directed the City Solicitor to prepare the amending bylaw.
briefs.action_required: Review updated Burnaby plumbing permit fee schedule before submitting any new permit applications.

briefs.title: City-imposed restrictive covenants on private properties can now be discharged on request
briefs.summary: Council approved the discharge of outdated City-imposed restrictive covenants on private properties, authorizing the City Solicitor to execute discharges upon owner request.
briefs.action_required: If your property has a City-imposed restrictive covenant, contact the City Solicitor's office to request its discharge.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Real Estate
